methodology.description: Conviction scores derived from five-gate screener + opportunity-universe framework. Capital preservation filter removes pre-FCF and explicitly flagged names. Opportunity-universe tickers inject pre-consensus picks with low institutional crowding even if scanner classifies them as NEAR_PEAK.
methodology.scoring: conviction_score = f(scanner_total_score) + signal_tier_bonus + macro_theme_bonus; OR f(baseScore * 0.92) for OPP_UNIVERSE injections. Range 40–95.
methodology.hard_rejects: Tickers within 8% of 52wH or revenue declining >5% YoY removed by scanner unless present in opportunity-universe framework list.

lean_into: Infrastructure beneficiaries with durable demand pull
Second-order AI systems and power bottlenecks
Financial rails benefiting from digitization and treasury transition
macro_context.posture_note: GET IN EARLY: Pre-consensus positions with low institutional crowding. Capital preservation first — FCF positive, no binary risk, clear invalidation defined.

pullback_context.posture_on_pullback: RESEARCH_SCREENED: All candidates passed 5-gate screening. Verify thesis before sizing any position.
pullback_context.data_note: Scanner evaluates revenue growth, P/S, P/FCF, dislocation depth, moat, and insider buying. Entry requires thesis verification — price action alone is not sufficient.

top10.name: Broadcom
top10.decline_explanation: Revenue growing 23.9% while stock is 24.5% below its high — the fundamentals and price are moving in opposite directions. This is the setup.
fundamental_signals: FCF $26914M (42.1% FCF margin) — strong cash generation
Level-1 supply chain — core/obvious play, moat real but widely known
gaps: P/S 27.7× — very expensive, high conviction needed
top10.moat_summary: Deep hyperscaler relationships. Custom ASIC design is irreplaceable once embedded — hyperscalers design multi-year chip roadmaps around AVGO.
top10.timing_note: Watch: Q3 FY2026: custom ASIC pipeline + AI rev guide
top10.next_catalyst: Q3 FY2026: custom ASIC pipeline + AI rev guide

top10.name: BWX Technologies
top10.decline_explanation: Revenue growing 26.1% while stock is 24.1% below its high — the fundamentals and price are moving in opposite directions. This is the setup.
fundamental_signals: Level-2 supply chain — underappreciated, 2-4 competitors, high switching costs
gaps: Gross margin 0% — thin, possible commodity risk
P/S not computable (missing shares or revenue)
top10.moat_summary: Legal monopoly. Federal law requires US Navy nuclear propulsion components from BWXT — no substitute, no foreign alternative permitted. Zero AI disruption risk. Not a speculative SMR play — mandated defense infrastructure with revenue pre-sold.
top10.next_catalyst: Additional naval nuclear contract awards throughout 2026. Q2 and Q3 2026 earnings confirming 60%+ backlog-to-revenue conversion.
top10.early_entry_signal: Market prices BWXT as a cyclical industrial compounder. Correct read: legally mandated defense utility with 2.3x annual revenue in contracted backlog already signed. The $8.65B is not speculative — it is executed contracts. SMR upside is free optionality on top. The narrative says nuclear is speculative; the data says this specific company has revenue pre-sold through 2027.
top10.invalidation: Defense budget cut >20% to naval nuclear programs. Quarterly revenue falls below $870M for two consecutive quarters — signals backlog conversion is slowing.

top10.name: Kinsale Capital
top10.decline_explanation: Revenue growing 10.2% but the market is derating the multiple — likely rate environment or growth-rate deceleration from a prior peak. Valuation now matters.
fundamental_signals: Standalone moat — not in supply chain hierarchy
gaps: Gross margin 0% — thin, possible commodity risk
P/S not computable (missing shares or revenue)
top10.moat_summary: Proprietary actuarial data and technology-first underwriting culture built over 15+ years of loss history. 77.4% combined ratio cannot be replicated by a new entrant — it requires 15 years of data discipline. Low expense ratio from technology-driven operations. The moat compounds every year the database grows.
top10.timing_note: Watch: Q2 2026 combined ratio below 80% confirms underwriting quality intact through E&S softening
top10.next_catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings: combined ratio confirmation below 80% proves underwriting quality intact. E&S market pricing stabilization data from industry reports.
top10.early_entry_signal: Nobody talks about insurance in an AI bull market. KNSL is down 24% with no deterioration in underwriting quality — the dislocation is narrative, not data. This is precisely the Buffett entry template: boring business, down for sentiment reasons, best-in-class economics compounding at 15%+ for a decade, high FCF, no AI disruption risk. The crowd will never arrive here during an AI bull market — which is exactly when you buy it.
top10.invalidation: Combined ratio rises above 85% for two consecutive quarters — signals competitive pressure has eroded underwriting standards. Premium growth falls below 0% YoY — structural market share loss.

top10.name: ASML Holding
top10.moat_summary: Absolute monopoly. 20+ years to replicate. Proprietary technology developed over decades.
top10.action_permission: RESEARCH_SCREENED — pre-consensus opportunity-universe pick. Crowding: unknown

top10.name: Energy Transfer LP
fundamental_signals: Pipeline companies are entirely outside the AI investment narrative. The connection is simple: AI data centers consume enormous natural gas for power generation; natural gas travels by pipeline; ET has $25B in contractual fee revenue tied to this demand over 18 years. The market classifies ET as an energy stock — it is actually an AI infrastructure income vehicle. 7-8% yield provides income while waiting for the narrative to catch up.
top10.moat_summary: 90,000+ miles of physical pipeline infrastructure that cannot be replicated. Contracted fee revenue — payment is not contingent on gas prices but on gas volumes flowing through the pipe. AI data centers cannot run without natural gas; ET owns the pipes.
top10.timing_note: Watch: Q2 2026 DCF confirms AI data center gas volume ramp — $25B contracted fee revenue
top10.next_catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings: confirmation of AI data center contract revenue ramp. Any new major hyperscaler gas supply contract announced publicly. Natural gas policy tailwinds from AI energy demand narrative.
top10.action_permission: RESEARCH_SCREENED — pre-consensus opportunity-universe pick. Crowding: low
top10.early_entry_signal: Pipeline companies are entirely outside the AI investment narrative. The connection is simple: AI data centers consume enormous natural gas for power generation; natural gas travels by pipeline; ET has $25B in contractual fee revenue tied to this demand over 18 years. The market classifies ET as an energy stock — it is actually an AI infrastructure income vehicle. 7-8% yield provides income while waiting for the narrative to catch up.
top10.invalidation: Hyperscaler AI capex cut >20% reduces gas demand growth. Distribution cut — signals cash flow stress. Two consecutive quarters of DCF decline QoQ.

top10.moat_summary: Deep hyperscaler manufacturing integration — multi-year supply agreements with design-to-manufacturing integration create switching costs. CLS is embedded in hardware design cycles, not just assembly. Specialized clean-room manufacturing capability.
top10.timing_note: Watch: Q2 2026 FY guide raised above $19B — AI hardware manufacturing demand confirmation
top10.next_catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings: FY guide raised further above $19B confirms AI hardware demand acceleration. Major analyst initiation at bulge-bracket bank would force institutional recognition.
top10.early_entry_signal: Druckenmiller-style early entry: same AI infrastructure demand as AVGO, but no major fund showed significant new CLS position in Q1 2026 13F filings. +53% revenue growth with $19B FY guide raised — the crowd hasn't arrived. Trades at 18x forward PE while AVGO trades at 32x for the same demand tailwind. The mispricing exists because CLS is classified as a boring contract manufacturer, not an AI infrastructure name.
top10.invalidation: Hyperscaler AI capex cut >15%. Loss of a top-2 customer relationship disclosed in earnings. Revenue growth drops below 20% for two consecutive quarters — signals the AI hardware manufacturing boom is plateauing.

ranked.name: GE Aerospace
fundamental_signals: Aviation supercycle is undervalued relative to AI capex narrative — institutional attention is entirely on semiconductors. The $211B backlog is not speculative: airlines have paid deposits on planes that require GE engines. The narrative says 'industrials already ran' — the data says backlog keeps growing every quarter. Entry requires a pullback to ~35x forward PE or earnings-driven dislocation.
ranked.moat_summary: Jet engine technology requires 20+ years to develop and another decade for FAA certification. The LEAP engine backlog represents 10+ years of contracted production. Service revenue on the installed engine fleet (aftermarket parts + maintenance) is recurring and long-duration — every engine sold creates a service annuity.
ranked.timing_note: Watch: Q2 2026 backlog growth + FCF trajectory — aviation supercycle confirmation
ranked.next_catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings: backlog growth confirmation + FCF trajectory update. Boeing production ramp — any acceleration in Boeing 737 MAX deliveries directly improves GE engine delivery cadence and revenue recognition.
ranked.action_permission: RESEARCH_SCREENED — pre-consensus opportunity-universe pick. Crowding: medium
ranked.early_entry_signal: Aviation supercycle is undervalued relative to AI capex narrative — institutional attention is entirely on semiconductors. The $211B backlog is not speculative: airlines have paid deposits on planes that require GE engines. The narrative says 'industrials already ran' — the data says backlog keeps growing every quarter. Entry requires a pullback to ~35x forward PE or earnings-driven dislocation.
ranked.invalidation: Backlog declines for two consecutive quarters — demand saturation signal. FCF misses FY guidance by >15%. Boeing production rate cut that directly reduces LEAP engine orders in the near term.
